title: crataegus mackenzii var. aspera
intro: Crataegus mackenzii var. aspera, also known as the rough-leaved hawthorn, is a variety of the hawthorn plant, a type of shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, such as quercetin and oligonol, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective effects. The health benefits of Crataegus mackenzii var. aspera include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage, making it a promising natural remedy for heart health and overall well-being. Crataegus mackenzii var. aspera can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, and it is often combined with other herbal remedies to enhance its therapeutic effects.
